label: Sterkfontein Caves
sub: UNESCO Cradle of Humankind; hominid fossils dating 2.5M+ years including 'Mrs. Ples' and 'Little Foot'

label: Mapungubwe Hill
sub: UNESCO site; capital of southern Africa's first known state (~900–1300 CE). Golden rhinoceros figurine found here.

label: Cape of Good Hope
sub: Rounded by Bartolomeu Dias in 1488; named by João II. Gateway to the Indian Ocean trade route.

label: Cape Town – Castle of Good Hope
sub: VOC refreshment station founded 1652 by Jan van Riebeeck; oldest surviving building in South Africa and seat of colonial administration.

label: Robben Island
sub: UNESCO site; political prison where Nelson Mandela spent 18 years. Symbol of apartheid repression and endurance.

label: Victor Verster Prison (Paarl)
sub: Where Mandela spent his final 2 years of imprisonment. He walked free from these gates on 11 February 1990, watched by 1 billion worldwide.

label: Isandlwana Battlefield
sub: 22 January 1879: Zulu army destroyed British force of ~1,300 — worst British defeat of the 19th century. National Heritage Site.

label: Blood River / Ncome
sub: 16 December 1838: Voortrekkers defeated Zulu force; site of contested Afrikaner nationalist mythology. Ncome Museum opened in 1999.

label: Ulundi
sub: Former Zulu royal capital; Battle of Ulundi (4 July 1879) ended the Anglo-Zulu War and shattered Zulu kingdom independence.

label: Kimberley – The Big Hole
sub: Diamonds discovered 1867; home of De Beers. World's largest hand-dug excavation; catalyst for British annexation and the mineral revolution.

label: Johannesburg
sub: Founded 1886 after Witwatersrand gold discovery; largest city and economic hub. Hosts Constitutional Court (Constitution Hill) and Chamber of Mines.

label: Liliesleaf Farm (Rivonia)
sub: ANC underground headquarters raided July 1963; arrests led to Rivonia Trial and Mandela's life sentence. Now a heritage museum.

label: Pretoria – Union Buildings
sub: Seat of the South African government; site of Mandela's 1994 inauguration. Built 1909–1913; bronze Mandela statue overlooks the gardens.

label: Soweto – Hector Pieterson Memorial
sub: 16 June 1976 Soweto Uprising; Hector Pieterson (12–13) was one of the first killed. Sam Nzima's photograph became a symbol of apartheid brutality.

label: Sharpeville Memorial
sub: 21 March 1960: Police shot 69 protesters against pass laws; 180+ wounded. Now Human Rights Day. UNESCO Memory of the World register.

label: Marikana
sub: 16 August 2012: Police killed 34 striking Lonmin platinum miners. Farlam Commission found police planned the massacre. National crisis point.

label: Bloemfontein – ANC Founding
sub: 8 January 1912: SANNC (later ANC) founded at Waaihoek Wesleyan Church by John Langalibalele Dube, Pixley ka Isaka Seme, and others.

label: Durban (eThekwini)
sub: Major port city; centre of South African Indian community. Site of 1949 Durban race riots; key IFP stronghold in political transition era.

label: Nkandla
sub: Jacob Zuma's private homestead; R246m in state funds spent on upgrades. Constitutional Court ruled Zuma violated oath (2016). Symbol of state capture.

label: Pietermaritzburg
sub: Founded 1838 by Voortrekkers; capital of KwaZulu-Natal. Where Gandhi was thrown off a train (1893) — radicalising his political consciousness.

label: Kliptown – Freedom Charter
sub: 25–26 June 1955: Congress of the People adopted the Freedom Charter — foundational document of liberation movement. Walter Sisulu Square now on site.

label: Pollsmoor Prison
sub: Mandela held here 1982–1988 after transfer from Robben Island. Desmond Tutu and others met Mandela here; early secret government contacts made.

label: Port Elizabeth / Gqeberha – Biko
sub: Steve Biko's home city; died in security police custody 12 September 1977. Steve Biko Foundation and memorial located here.

label: uKhahlamba-Drakensberg
sub: UNESCO site; 35,000+ San rock art images in 600+ sites — world's most concentrated collection. Some images over 3,000 years old.

label: Constitution Hill
sub: Former Old Fort Prison (held Mandela, Gandhi) transformed into the Constitutional Court (2004). Symbol of transition from repression to democracy.
